title: Privacy Act of 1974; System of Records
raw_text:
Privacy Act of 1974; System of Records

In accordance with the Privacy Act of 1974 and Office of Management and Budget ("OMB") Circular A-108, the Securities and Exchange Commission ("Commission" or "SEC") is proposing a modification to its system of records notice (SORN), SEC-06, SEC's Financial and Acquisition Management System ("SEC-06"). This proposed update introduces a new routine use to comply with Executive Order (E.O.) 14249, Protecting America's Bank Account Against Fraud, Waste, and Abuse. The change supports enhanced efforts to safeguard federal financial systems and ensure responsible stewardship of public funds. The modified SORN does not change the categories of individuals, the record collection process, the authorities, or the purpose of collection. It also does not affect individuals' rights to access or amend their records under the Privacy Act.

raw_text:
MIAX Emerald — Regulatory Alert, Trading Alert — Business Continuity and Disaster Recovery Plans Testing and Member Designation Standards
Regulatory Alert, Trading Alert MIAX Emerald December 31, 2025 | 11:08 AM Business Continuity and Disaster Recovery Plans Testing and Member Designation Standards As required by Regulation Systems Compliance and Integrity and MIAX Options Rule 321, which is incorporated by reference into the MIAX Pearl, MIAX Emerald and MIAX Sapphire Exchange Rule Books, certain Members of the MIAX Exchanges will have mandatory participation requirements in the annual SIFMA Business Continuity Planning Disaster Recovery (“BC/DR”) test. Please refer to the following Regulatory Circulars for more information on BC/DR designation standards and requirements for Designated Members: MIAX Options RC 2025-107 MIAX Pearl Options RC 2025-109 MIAX Emerald Options RC 2025-106 MIAX Sapphire Options RC 2025-129 MIAX Pearl Equities RC 2025-14 Please direct any requests for further information regarding BC/DR testing to TradingOperations@miaxglobal.com or (609) 897-7302. Regulatory questions may be directed to Regulatory@miaxglobal.com or (609) 897-7309.

title: MIAX Pearl Equities Exchange January 1, 2026 Fee Changes
raw_text:
MIAX Pearl Equities — Fee Change Alert — MIAX Pearl Equities Exchange January 1, 2026 Fee Changes
Fee Change Alert MIAX Pearl Equities December 31, 2025 | 01:59 PM MIAX Pearl Equities Exchange January 1, 2026 Fee Changes Effective January 1, 2026, pending filing with the SEC, MIAX Pearl Equities will introduce a new Market Quoting Program. Firms whose "Percent Time at NBBO" is greater than or equal to 50% in an average of at least 750 securities per day for the month will receive an enhanced rebate of ($0.0026) per share for executions resulting from adding displayed liquidity in securities priced at or above $1.00. Additional details for the Market Quoting Program include the following: The minimum threshold of an average of at least 750 securities per day can be achieved from all multi-listed securities, not just those on the Market Quality Securities list. Firms who qualify for the Market Quoting Program and receive this rebate will NOT be eligible to receive the NBBO Setter Additive Rebate or the NBBO First Joiner Additive Rebate. Firms who qualify for the Market Quoting Program and the NBBO Setter Plus Program will receive the higher rebate offered between the two programs. Only performance during the Regular Trading Session will count towards a Firm’s eligibility in the Market Quoting Program. Rebates for this program are applicable to the following Fee Codes: Liquidity Code AA, “Adds Liquidity, Displayed Order (Tape A)” Liquidity Code AB, “Adds Liquidity, Displayed Order (Tape B)” Liquidity Code AC, “Adds Liquidity, Displayed Order (Tape C)” Liquidity Code EA, “Early Trading Session, Adds Liquidity, Displayed Order (Tape A)” Liquidity Code EB, “Early Trading Session, Adds Liquidity, Displayed Order (Tape B)” Liquidity Code EC, “Early Trading Session, Adds Liquidity, Displayed Order (Tape C)” Liquidity Code FA, “Late Trading Session, Adds Liquidity, Displayed Order (Tape A)” Liquidity Code FB, “Late Trading Session, Adds Liquidity, Displayed Order (Tape B)” Liquidity Code FC, “Late Trading Session, Adds Liquidity, Displayed Order (Tape C)” The updated Market Quality Securities list, effective January 1, 2026, is posted on the MIAX website. title: MIAX Pearl Equities Exchange - Update to Support SEC Fee Transparency

MIAX Pearl Equities — Fee Change Alert — MIAX Pearl Equities Exchange - Update to Support SEC Fee Transparency
Fee Change Alert MIAX Pearl Equities December 30, 2025 | 01:00 PM MIAX Pearl Equities Exchange - Update to Support SEC Fee Transparency Effective February 1, 2026, pending filing with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC), MIAX Pearl Equities will adopt a new billing methodology in alignment with Regulation NMS Rule 610(d) amendments announced by the SEC. On the effective date, MIAX Pearl Equities will begin calculating pricing tiers for the current month based on the prior month’s trading activity. For example, February tier qualifications will be based on trading activity in January. New Equity Members of MIAX Pearl Equities will default to base pricing tiers as no prior month activity will be available. To aid Equity Members in understanding their rates for a particular month, MIAX Pearl Equities will make multiple enhancements to its Monthly Tier Report based on the changes described above. title: NYSE Options: 2026 Penny Program Annual Rebalance
raw_text:
NYSE Options: 2026 Penny Program Annual Rebalance
Markets: NYSE AMERICAN OPTIONS, NYSE ARCA OPTIONS; Services: Trading
Effective January 2, 2026 , NYSE American Options and NYSE Arca Options ("NYSE Options", each an "Exchange") will update the list of issues in the Penny Interval Program for Options (the "Penny Program") pursuant to the Annual Review provided for in the Penny Program, as described below. Detailed changes to Penny Program issues can be found here . Penny Program Annual Review NYSE Options adopted the Penny Program on July 1, 2020, and pursuant to the same, an annual rebalancing is performed wherein the Exchange adjusts the Penny Program to reflect changes in trading activity since it was adopted. The rebalancing provides: Additions to the Penny Program: Based on the Annual Review, any option class not in the Penny Program that is among the 300 most actively traded multiply listed option classes overlying securities priced below $200, or an index at an index level below $200, will be added to the Penny Program on the first trading day of January. Removals from the Penny Program: Except as provided for issues subject to corporate action or having been added since the last rebalancing, based on the Annual Review, any option class in the Penny Program that falls outside the 425 most actively traded multiply listed option classes will be removed from the Penny Program on the first trading day of April. Any option class added during the year because of activity following initial listing will be added on the first trading day of the month after it qualifies and will remain in the Penny Program for one full calendar year. Any series in an option class participating in the Penny Program in which the underlying security has been delisted or are identified by OCC as ineligible for opening customer transactions, will continue to quote pursuant to the terms of the Penny Program until all such options have expired. For more information about the Penny Program, see NYSE American Rule 960.1NY and NYSE Arca Rule 6.72A-O. Contact Information Questions regarding the Penny Interval Program for Options may be directed to the NYSE Trading Operations Desk at 212-896-2830 option 1, 2. For any questions regarding this update, please contact rmteam@nyse.com .

title: NYSE Arca - Updated Limit Up Limit Down Tier 1 ETP List
raw_text:
NYSE Arca - Updated Limit Up Limit Down Tier 1 ETP List
Markets: NYSE ARCA EQUITIES; Services: Trading
NYSE Arca, in coordination with other listing exchanges, has updated the list of Exchange Traded Products eligible for Tier 1 of the Limit Up Limit Down program. The updated list of ETPs, effective January 2, 2026, as well as the additions and deletions from the current Tier 1 list, can be found here . To determine eligibility as a Tier 1 NMS stock, all ETPs across multiple asset classes and issuers were sorted by notional consolidated average daily volume ("CADV"). The list included domestic equity, international equity, fixed income, currency, and commodities and futures but excluded leveraged ETPs. Notional CADV was calculated by multiplying daily volumes by closing prices each day of the measuring period and then averaging them for the period. The measurement period was the first day of the previous fiscal half year up until one week before the beginning of the next fiscal half year, in this case, 7/1/2025 to 12/22/2025. ETPs, including inverse ETPs, which traded over $2,000,000 CADV were eligible to be included as a Tier 1 NMS stock.
